NKJV.1: “To the angel of the church of Ephesus write, ‘These things says  He who holds the seven stars in His right hand,  who walks in the midst of the seven golden lampstands:
NKJV.2: “I know your works, your labor, your patience, and that you cannot bear those who are evil. And  you have tested those  who say they are apostles and are not, and have found them liars;
NKJV.3: and you have persevered and have patience, and have labored for My name’s sake and have  not become weary.
NKJV.4: Nevertheless I have this against you, that you have left your first love.
NKJV.5: Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent and do the first works,  or else I will come to you quickly and remove your lampstand from its place—unless you repent.
NKJV.6: But this you have, that you hate the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
NKJV.7: “He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes I will give  to eat from  the tree of life, which is in the midst of the Paradise of God.” ’
NKJV.8: “And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write, ‘These things says  the First and the Last, who was dead, and came to life:
NKJV.9: “I know your works, tribulation, and poverty (but you are rich); and I know the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
NKJV.10: Do not fear any of those things which you are about to suffer. Indeed, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested, and you will have tribulation ten days. Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life.
NKJV.11: “He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. He who overcomes shall not be hurt by  the second death.” ’
NKJV.12: “And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write, ‘These things says  He who has the sharp two-edged sword:
NKJV.13: “I know your works, and where you dwell, where Satan’s throne is. And you hold fast to My name, and did not deny My faith even in the days in which Antipas was My faithful martyr, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.
NKJV.14: But I have a few things against you, because you have there those who hold the doctrine of  Balaam, who taught Balak to put a stumbling block before the children of Israel,  to eat things sacrificed to idols,  and to commit sexual immorality.
NKJV.15: Thus you also have those who hold the doctrine of the Nicolaitans, which thing I hate.
NKJV.16: Repent, or else I will come to you quickly and  will fight against them with the sword of My mouth.
NKJV.17: “He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes I will give some of the hidden manna to eat. And I will give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name written which no one knows except him who receives it.” ’
NKJV.18: “And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write, ‘These things says the Son of God,  who has eyes like a flame of fire, and His feet like fine brass:
NKJV.19: “I know your works, love, service, faith, and your patience; and as for your works, the last are more than the first.
NKJV.20: Nevertheless I have a few things against you, because you allow that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, to teach and seduce My servants  to commit sexual immorality and eat things sacrificed to idols.
NKJV.21: And I gave her time  to repent of her sexual immorality, and she did not repent.
NKJV.22: Indeed I will cast her into a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of their deeds.
NKJV.23: I will kill her children with death, and all the churches shall know that I am He who  searches the minds and hearts. And I will give to each one of you according to your works.
NKJV.24: “Now to you I say, and to the rest in Thyatira, as many as do not have this doctrine, who have not known the  depths of Satan, as they say,  I will put on you no other burden.
NKJV.25: But hold fast  what you have till I come.
NKJV.26: And he who overcomes, and keeps  My works until the end,  to him I will give power over the nations—
NKJV.27: ‘He  shall rule them with a rod of iron; They shall be dashed to pieces like the potter’s vessels’—  as I also have received from My Father;
NKJV.28: and I will give him the morning star.
NKJV.29: “He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.” ’

ESV.1: “To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: ‘The words of him who holds the seven stars in his right hand, who walks among the seven golden lampstands.
ESV.2: “‘I know your works, your toil and your patient endurance, and how you cannot bear with those who are evil, but have tested those who call themselves apostles and are not, and found them to be false.
ESV.3: I know you are enduring patiently and bearing up for my name's sake, and you have not grown weary.
ESV.4: But I have this against you, that you have abandoned the love you had at first.
ESV.5: Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.
ESV.6: Yet this you have: you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
ESV.7: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers I will grant to eat of the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.’
ESV.8: “And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: ‘The words of the first and the last, who died and came to life.
ESV.9: “‘I know your tribulation and your poverty (but you are rich) and the slander  of those who say that they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
ESV.10: Do not fear what you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested, and for ten days you will have tribulation. Be faithful unto death, and I will give you the crown of life.
ESV.11: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who conquers will not be hurt by the second death.’
ESV.12: “And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: ‘The words of him who has the sharp two-edged sword.
ESV.13: “‘I know where you dwell, where Satan's throne is. Yet you hold fast my name, and you did not deny my faith  even in the days of Antipas my faithful witness, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.
ESV.14: But I have a few things against you: you have some there who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to put a stumbling block before the sons of Israel, so that they might eat food sacrificed to idols and practice sexual immorality.
ESV.15: So also you have some who hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
ESV.16: Therefore repent. If not, I will come to you soon and war against them with the sword of my mouth.
ESV.17: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers I will give some of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, with a new name written on the stone that no one knows except the one who receives it.’
ESV.18: “And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: ‘The words of the Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and whose feet are like burnished bronze.
ESV.19: “‘I know your works, your love and faith and service and patient endurance, and that your latter works exceed the first.
ESV.20: But I have this against you, that you tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess and is teaching and seducing my servants to practice sexual immorality and to eat food sacrificed to idols.
ESV.21: I gave her time to repent, but she refuses to repent of her sexual immorality.
ESV.22: Behold, I will throw her onto a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her I will throw into great tribulation, unless they repent of her works,
ESV.23: and I will strike her children dead. And all the churches will know that I am he who searches mind and heart, and I will give to each of you according to your works.
ESV.24: But to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold this teaching, who have not learned what some call the deep things of Satan, to you I say, I do not lay on you any other burden.
ESV.25: Only hold fast what you have until I come.
ESV.26: The one who conquers and who keeps my works until the end, to him I will give authority over the nations,
ESV.27: and he will rule  them with a rod of iron, as when earthen pots are broken in pieces, even as I myself have received authority from my Father.
ESV.28: And I will give him the morning star.
ESV.29: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.’

NASB.1: 'To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: The One who holds the seven stars in His right hand, the One who walks among the seven golden lampstands, says this:
NASB.2: 'I know your deeds and your toil and perseverance, and that you cannot tolerate evil men, and you put to the test those who call themselves apostles, and they are not, and you found them [to be] false;
NASB.3: and you have perseverance and have endured for My name's sake, and have not grown weary.
NASB.4: 'But I have [this] against you, that you have left your first love.
NASB.5: 'Therefore remember from where you have fallen, and repent and do the deeds you did at first; or else I am coming to you and will remove your lampstand out of its place-- unless you repent.
NASB.6: 'Yet this you do have, that you hate the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
NASB.7: 'He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes, I will grant to eat of the tree of life which is in the Paradise of God.'
NASB.8: 'And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: The first and the last, who was dead, and has come to life, says this:
NASB.9: 'I know your tribulation and your poverty (but you are rich), and the blasphemy by those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
NASB.10: 'Do not fear what you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to cast some of you into prison, so that you will be tested, and you will have tribulation for ten days. Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life.
NASB.11: 'He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. He who overcomes will not be hurt by the second death.'
NASB.12: 'And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: The One who has the sharp two-edged sword says this:
NASB.13: 'I know where you dwell, where Satan's throne is; and you hold fast My name, and did not deny My faith even in the days of Antipas, My witness, My faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.
NASB.14: 'But I have a few things against you, because you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who kept teaching Balak to put a stumbling block before the sons of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols and to commit [acts of] immorality.
NASB.15: 'So you also have some who in the same way hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
NASB.16: 'Therefore repent; or else I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of My mouth.
NASB.17: 'He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes, to him I will give [some] of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, and a new name written on the stone which no one knows but he who receives it.'
NASB.18: 'And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: The Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and His feet are like burnished bronze, says this:
NASB.19: 'I know your deeds, and your love and faith and service and perseverance, and that your deeds of late are greater than at first.
NASB.20: 'But I have [this] against you, that you tolerate the woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, and she teaches and leads My bond-servants astray so that they commit [acts of] immorality and eat things sacrificed to idols.
NASB.21: 'I gave her time to repent, and she does not want to repent of her immorality.
NASB.22: 'Behold, I will throw her on a bed [of sickness], and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of her deeds.
NASB.23: 'And I will kill her children with pestilence, and all the churches will know that I am He who searches the minds and hearts; and I will give to each one of you according to your deeds.
NASB.24: 'But I say to you, the rest who are in Thyatira, who do not hold this teaching, who have not known the deep things of Satan, as they call them-- I place no other burden on you.
NASB.25: 'Nevertheless what you have, hold fast until I come.
NASB.26: 'He who overcomes, and he who keeps My deeds until the end, TO HIM I WILL GIVE AUTHORITY OVER THE NATIONS;
NASB.27: AND HE SHALL RULE THEM WITH A ROD OF IRON, AS THE VESSELS OF THE POTTER ARE BROKEN TO PIECES, as I also have received [authority] from My Father;
NASB.28: and I will give him the morning star.
NASB.29: 'He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.'

NLT.1: “Write this letter to the angel of the church in Ephesus. This is the message from the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand, the one who walks among the seven gold lampstands:
NLT.2: “I know all the things you do. I have seen your hard work and your patient endurance. I know you don’t tolerate evil people. You have examined the claims of those who say they are apostles but are not. You have discovered they are liars.
NLT.3: You have patiently suffered for me without quitting.
NLT.4: “But I have this complaint against you. You don’t love me or each other as you did at first!
NLT.5: Look how far you have fallen! Turn back to me and do the works you did at first. If you don’t repent, I will come and remove your lampstand from its place among the churches.
NLT.6: But this is in your favor: You hate the evil deeds of the Nicolaitans, just as I do.
NLT.7: “Anyone with ears to hear must listen to the Spirit and understand what he is saying to the churches. To everyone who is victorious I will give fruit from the tree of life in the paradise of God.
NLT.8: “Write this letter to the angel of the church in Smyrna. This is the message from the one who is the First and the Last, who was dead but is now alive:
NLT.9: “I know about your suffering and your poverty — but you are rich! I know the blasphemy of those opposing you. They say they are Jews, but they are not, because their synagogue belongs to Satan.
NLT.10: Don’t be afraid of what you are about to suffer. The devil will throw some of you into prison to test you. You will suffer for ten days. But if you remain faithful even when facing death, I will give you the crown of life.
NLT.11: “Anyone with ears to hear must listen to the Spirit and understand what he is saying to the churches. Whoever is victorious will not be harmed by the second death.
NLT.12: “Write this letter to the angel of the church in Pergamum. This is the message from the one with the sharp two-edged sword:
NLT.13: “I know that you live in the city where Satan has his throne, yet you have remained loyal to me. You refused to deny me even when Antipas, my faithful witness, was martyred among you there in Satan’s city.
NLT.14: “But I have a few complaints against you. You tolerate some among you whose teaching is like that of Balaam, who showed Balak how to trip up the people of Israel. He taught them to sin by eating food offered to idols and by committing sexual sin.
NLT.15: In a similar way, you have some Nicolaitans among you who follow the same teaching.
NLT.16: Repent of your sin, or I will come to you suddenly and fight against them with the sword of my mouth.
NLT.17: “Anyone with ears to hear must listen to the Spirit and understand what he is saying to the churches. To everyone who is victorious I will give some of the manna that has been hidden away in heaven. And I will give to each one a white stone, and on the stone will be engraved a new name that no one understands except the one who receives it.
NLT.18: “Write this letter to the angel of the church in Thyatira. This is the message from the Son of God, whose eyes are like flames of fire, whose feet are like polished bronze:
NLT.19: “I know all the things you do. I have seen your love, your faith, your service, and your patient endurance. And I can see your constant improvement in all these things.
NLT.20: “But I have this complaint against you. You are permitting that woman — that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet — to lead my servants astray. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols.
NLT.21: I gave her time to repent, but she does not want to turn away from her immorality.
NLT.22: “Therefore, I will throw her on a bed of suffering, and those who commit adultery with her will suffer greatly unless they repent and turn away from her evil deeds.
NLT.23: I will strike her children dead. Then all the churches will know that I am the one who searches out the thoughts and intentions of every person. And I will give to each of you whatever you deserve.
NLT.24: “But I also have a message for the rest of you in Thyatira who have not followed this false teaching (‘deeper truths,’ as they call them — depths of Satan, actually). I will ask nothing more of you
NLT.25: except that you hold tightly to what you have until I come.
NLT.26:
To all who are victorious, who obey me to the very end,
To them I will give authority over all the nations.
NLT.27:
They will rule the nations with an iron rod
and smash them like clay pots.
NLT.28: They will have the same authority I received from my Father, and I will also give them the morning star!
NLT.29: “Anyone with ears to hear must listen to the Spirit and understand what he is saying to the churches.

WEB.1: “To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write:  “He who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks among the seven golden lamp stands says these things:
WEB.2: “I know your works, and your toil and perseverance, and that you can’t tolerate evil men, and have tested those who call themselves apostles, and they are not, and found them false.
WEB.3: You have perseverance and have endured for my name’s sake, and have   not grown weary.
WEB.4: But I have this against you, that you left your first love.
WEB.5: Remember therefore from where you have fallen, and repent and do the first works; or else I am coming to you swiftly, and will move your lamp stand out of its place, unless you repent.
WEB.6: But this you have, that you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
WEB.7: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes I will give to eat from the tree of life, which is in the Paradise of my God.
WEB.8: “To the angel of the assembly in Smyrna write:  “The first and the last, who was dead, and has come to life says these things:
WEB.9: “I know your works, oppression, and your poverty (but you are rich), and the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
WEB.10: Don’t be afraid of the things which you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested; and you will have oppression for ten days. Be faithful to death, and I will give you the crown of life.
WEB.11: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. He who overcomes won’t be harmed by the second death.
WEB.12: “To the angel of the assembly in Pergamum write:  “He who has the sharp two-edged sword says these things:
WEB.13: “I know your works and where you dwell, where Satan’s throne is. You hold firmly to my name, and didn’t deny my faith in the days of Antipas my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.
WEB.14: But I have a few things against you, because you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to throw a stumbling block before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols, and to commit sexual immorality.
WEB.15: So you also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans likewise.
WEB.16: Repent therefore, or else I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of my mouth.
WEB.17: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes, to him I will give of the hidden manna,   and I will give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name written, which no one knows but he who receives it.
WEB.18: “To the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write:  “The Son of God, who has his eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet are like burnished brass, says these things:
WEB.19: “I know your works, your love, faith, service, patient endurance, and that your last works are more than the first.
WEB.20: But I have this against you, that you tolerate your  woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess. She teaches and seduces my servants to commit sexual immorality, and to eat things sacrificed to idols.
WEB.21: I gave her time to repent, but she refuses to repent of her sexual immorality.
WEB.22: Behold, I will throw her into a bed, and those who commit adultery with her into great oppression, unless they repent of her works.
WEB.23: I will kill her children with Death, and all the assemblies will know that I am he who searches the minds and hearts. I will give to each one of you according to your deeds.
WEB.24: But to you I say, to the rest who are in Thyatira, as many as don’t have this teaching, who don’t know what some call ‘the deep things of Satan,’ to you I say, I am not putting any other burden on you.
WEB.25: Nevertheless, hold that which you have firmly until I come.
WEB.26: He who overcomes, and he who keeps my works to the end, to him I will give authority over the nations.
WEB.27: He will rule them with a rod of iron, shattering them like clay pots;  as I also have received of my Father:
WEB.28: and I will give him the morning star.
WEB.29: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

CSB17.1: “Write to the angel <sup>[1]</sup> of the church in Ephesus: Thus says the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and who walks among the seven golden lampstands: <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance, <sup>ⓖ</sup> and that you cannot tolerate evil people. You have tested those who call themselves apostles and are not, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and you have found them to be liars. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: I know that you have persevered and endured hardships for the sake of my name, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and have not grown weary.
CSB17.4: But I have this against you: You have abandoned the love you had at first. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.5: Remember then how far you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. <sup>ⓛ</sup> Otherwise, I will come to you <sup>[2]</sup> and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.
CSB17.6: Yet you do have this: You hate the practices of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
CSB17.7: “Let anyone who has ears to hear listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will give <sup>ⓜ</sup> the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in <sup>[3]</sup> the paradise of God. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.8: “Write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: Thus says the First and the Last, <sup>ⓐ</sup> the one who was dead and came to life:
CSB17.9: I know your <sup>[4]</sup>affliction and poverty, but you are rich. I know the slander of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.10: Don’t be afraid of what you are about to suffer. Look, the devil <sup>ⓒ</sup>is about to throw some of you into prison to test you, and you will experience affliction for ten days. <sup>ⓓ</sup>Be faithful to the point of death, <sup>ⓔ</sup>and I will give you the crown <sup>[5]</sup>of life. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.11: “Let anyone who has ears to hear listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who conquers will never be harmed by the second death. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.12: “Write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: Thus says the one who has the sharp, double-edged sword:  <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.13: I know <sup>[6]</sup>where you live — where Satan’s throne is. Yet you are holding on to my name and did not deny your faith in me, <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>even in the days of Antipas, my faithful witness <sup>ⓙ</sup>who was put to death among you, where Satan lives.
CSB17.14: But I have a few things against you. You have some there who hold to the teaching of Balaam, <sup>ⓚ</sup>who taught Balak to place a stumbling block <sup>[8]</sup>in front of the Israelites: to eat meat sacrificed to idols <sup>ⓛ</sup>and to commit sexual immorality. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.15: In the same way, you also have those who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans. <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.16: So repent! Otherwise, I will come to you quickly <sup>ⓝ</sup>and fight against them with the sword of my mouth. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.17: “Let anyone who has ears to hear listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will give some of the hidden manna. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> I will also give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name <sup>ⓒ</sup> is inscribed that no one knows except the one who receives it.
CSB17.18: “Write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: Thus says the Son of God, <sup>ⓓ</sup> the one whose eyes are like a fiery flame and whose feet are like fine bronze: <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.19: I know your works — your love, faithfulness, <sup>[11]</sup>service, and endurance. <sup>ⓕ</sup>I know that your last works are greater than the first.
CSB17.20: But I have this against you: You tolerate the woman Jezebel, <sup>ⓖ</sup>who calls herself a prophetess and teaches and deceives my servants to commit sexual immorality and to eat meat sacrificed to idols. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.21: I gave her time to repent, <sup>ⓘ</sup>but she does not want to repent of her sexual immorality. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.22: Look, I will throw her into a sickbed <sup>ⓚ</sup>and those who commit adultery with her <sup>ⓛ</sup>into great affliction. <sup>ⓜ</sup>Unless they repent of her <sup>[12]</sup>works,
CSB17.23: I will strike her children dead. <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>Then all the churches will know that I am the one who examines minds and hearts, <sup>ⓐ</sup>and I will give to each of you according to your works. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.24: I say to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold this teaching, who haven’t known “the so-called secrets <sup>[14]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>of Satan” <sup>ⓓ</sup>— as they say — I am not putting any other burden on you. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.25: Only hold on to what you have until I come. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.26: The one who conquers and who keeps my works to the end: I will give him authority over the nations —
CSB17.27:
and he will rule [15] ⓖ them with an iron scepter;
he will shatter them like pottery [16] ⓗ —
CSB17.28: just as I have received this from my Father. I will also give him the morning star. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.29: “Let anyone who has ears to hear listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.

AMP.1:
"To the angel (divine messenger) of the church in Ephesus write:
"These are the words of the One who holds [firmly] the seven stars [which are the angels or messengers of the seven churches] in His right hand, the One who walks among the seven golden lampstands (the seven churches):
AMP.2: 'I know your deeds and your toil, and your patient endurance, and that you cannot tolerate those who are evil, and have tested and critically appraised those who call themselves apostles (special messengers, personally chosen representatives, of Christ), and [in fact] are not, and have found them to be liars and impostors;
AMP.3: and [I know that] you [who believe] are enduring patiently and are bearing up for My name's sake, and that you have not grown weary [of being faithful to the truth].
AMP.4: But I have this [charge] against you, that you have left your first love [you have lost the depth of love that you first had for Me].
AMP.5: So remember the heights from which you have fallen, and repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, your sinful behavior—seek God's will] and do the works you did at first [when you first knew Me]; otherwise, I will visit you and remove your lampstand (the church, its impact) from its place—unless you repent.
AMP.6: Yet you have this [to your credit], that you hate the works and corrupt teachings of the Nicolaitans [that mislead and delude the people], which I also hate.
AMP.7: He who has an ear, let him hear and heed what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes [the world through believing that Jesus is the Son of God], I will grant [the privilege] to eat [the fruit] from the tree of life, which is in the Paradise of God.'
AMP.8:
"And to the angel (divine messenger) of the church in Smyrna write:
"These are the words of the First and the Last [absolute Deity, the Son of God] who died and came to life [again]:
AMP.9: 'I know your suffering and your poverty (but you are rich), and how you are blasphemed and slandered by those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan [they are Jews only by blood, and do not believe and truly honor the God whom they claim to worship].
AMP.10: Fear nothing that you are about to suffer. Be aware that the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested [in your faith], and for ten days you will have tribulation. Be faithful to the point of death [if you must die for your faith], and I will give you the crown [consisting] of life.
AMP.11: He who has an ear, let him hear and heed what the Spirit says to the churches. He who overcomes [the world through believing that Jesus is the Son of God] will not be hurt by the second death ( the lake of fire).'
AMP.12:
"And to the angel (divine messenger) of the church in Pergamum write:
"These are the words of Him who has and wields the sharp two-edged sword [in judgment]:
AMP.13: 'I know where you dwell, [a place] where Satan sits enthroned. Yet you are holding fast to My name, and you did not deny My faith even in the days of Antipas, My witness, My faithful one, who was killed (martyred) among you, where Satan dwells.
AMP.14: But I have a few things against you, because you have there some [among you] who are holding to the [corrupt] teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to put a stumbling block before the sons of Israel, [enticing them] to eat things that had been sacrificed to idols and to commit [acts of sexual] immorality.
AMP.15: You also have some who in the same way are holding to the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
AMP.16: Therefore repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, your sinful behavior—seek God's will]; or else I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war and fight against them with the sword of My mouth [in judgment].
AMP.17: He who has an ear, let him hear and heed what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes [the world through believing that Jesus is the Son of God], to him I will give [the privilege of eating] some of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone with a new name engraved on the stone which no one knows except the one who receives it.'
AMP.18:
"And to the angel (divine messenger) of the church in Thyatira write:
"These are the words of the Son of God, who has eyes [that flash] like a flame of fire [in righteous judgment], and whose feet are like burnished [white-hot] bronze:
AMP.19: 'I know your deeds, your love and faith and service and patient endurance, and that your last deeds are more numerous and greater than the first.
AMP.20: But I have this [charge] against you, that you tolerate the woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess [claiming to be inspired], and she teaches and misleads My bond-servants so that they commit [acts of sexual] immorality and eat food sacrificed to idols.
AMP.21: I gave her time to repent [to change her inner self and her sinful way of thinking], but she has no desire to repent of her immorality and refuses to do so.
AMP.22: Listen carefully, I will throw her on a bed of sickness, and those who commit adultery with her [I will bring] into great anguish, unless they repent of her deeds.
AMP.23: And I will kill her children (followers) with pestilence [thoroughly annihilating them], and all the churches will know [without any doubt] that I am He who searches the minds and hearts [the innermost thoughts, purposes]; and I will give to each one of you [a reward or punishment] according to your deeds.
AMP.24: But to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold this teaching, who have not explored and known the depths of Satan, as they call them—I place no other burden on you,
AMP.25: except to hold tightly to what you have until I come.
AMP.26: And he who overcomes [the world through believing that Jesus is the Son of God] and he who keeps My deeds [doing things that please Me] until the [very] end, to him I will give authority and power over the nations;
AMP.27: and he shall shepherd and rule them with a rod of iron, as the earthen pots are broken in pieces, as I also have received authority [and power to rule them] from My Father;
AMP.28: and I will give him the Morning Star.
AMP.29: He who has an ear, let him hear and heed what the Spirit says to the churches.'

MSG.1: Write this to Ephesus, to the Angel of the church. The One with Seven Stars in his right-fist grip, striding through the golden seven-lights' circle, speaks:
MSG.2: "I see what you've done, your hard, hard work, your refusal to quit. I know you can't stomach evil, that you weed out apostolic pretenders.
MSG.3: I know your persistence, your courage in my cause, that you never wear out.
MSG.4: "But you walked away from your first love—why? What's going on with you, anyway?
MSG.5: Do you have any idea how far you've fallen? A Lucifer fall! "Turn back! Recover your dear early love. No time to waste, for I'm well on my way to removing your light from the golden circle.
MSG.6: "You do have this to your credit: You hate the Nicolaitan business. I hate it, too.
MSG.7: "Are your ears awake? Listen. Listen to the Wind Words, the Spirit blowing through the churches. I'm about to call each conqueror to dinner. I'm spreading a banquet of Tree-of-Life fruit, a supper plucked from God's orchard."
MSG.8: Write this to Smyrna, to the Angel of the church. The Beginning and Ending, the First and Final One, the Once Dead and Then Come Alive, speaks:
MSG.9: "I can see your pain and poverty—constant pain, dire poverty—but I also see your wealth. And I hear the lie in the claims of those who pretend to be good Jews, who in fact belong to Satan's crowd.
MSG.10: "Fear nothing in the things you're about to suffer—but stay on guard! Fear nothing! The Devil is about to throw you in jail for a time of testing—ten days. It won't last forever. "Don't quit, even if it costs you your life. Stay there believing. I have a Life-Crown sized and ready for you.
MSG.11: "Are your ears awake? Listen. Listen to the Wind Words, the Spirit blowing through the churches. Christ-conquerors are safe from Devil-death."
MSG.12: Write this to Pergamum, to the Angel of the church. The One with the sharp-biting sword draws from the sheath of his mouth—out come the sword words:
MSG.13: "I see where you live, right under the shadow of Satan's throne. But you continue boldly in my Name; you never once denied my Name, even when the pressure was worst, when they martyred Antipas, my witness who stayed faithful to me on Satan's turf.
MSG.14: "But why do you indulge that Balaam crowd? Don't you remember that Balaam was an enemy agent, seducing Balak and sabotaging Israel's holy pilgrimage by throwing unholy parties?
MSG.15: And why do you put up with the Nicolaitans, who do the same thing?
MSG.16: "Enough! Don't give in to them; I'll be with you soon. I'm fed up and about to cut them to pieces with my sword-sharp words.
MSG.17: "Are your ears awake? Listen. Listen to the Wind Words, the Spirit blowing through the churches. I'll give the sacred manna to every conqueror; I'll also give a clear, smooth stone inscribed with your new name, your secret new name."
MSG.18: Write this to Thyatira, to the Angel of the church. God's Son, eyes pouring fire-blaze, standing on feet of furnace-fired bronze, says this:
MSG.19: "I see everything you're doing for me. Impressive! The love and the faith, the service and persistence. Yes, very impressive! You get better at it every day.
MSG.20: "But why do you let that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet mislead my dear servants into Cross-denying, self-indulging religion?
MSG.21: I gave her a chance to change her ways, but she has no intention of giving up a career in the god-business.
MSG.22: I'm about to lay her low, along with her partners, as they play their sex-and-religion games.
MSG.23: The bastard offspring of their idol-whoring I'll kill. Then every church will know that appearances don't impress me. I x-ray every motive and make sure you get what's coming to you.
MSG.24: "The rest of you Thyatirans, who have nothing to do with this outrage, who scorn this playing around with the Devil that gets paraded as profundity, be assured I'll not make life any harder for you than it already is.
MSG.25: Hold on to the truth you have until I get there.
MSG.26: "Here's the reward I have for every conqueror, everyone who keeps at it, refusing to give up: You'll rule the nations,
MSG.27: your Shepherd-King rule as firm as an iron staff, their resistance fragile as clay pots. This was the gift my Father gave me; I pass it along to you—
MSG.28: and with it, the Morning Star!
MSG.29: "Are your ears awake? Listen. Listen to the Wind Words, the Spirit blowing through the churches."

NIV.1:
To the Church in Ephesus
“To the angel of the church in Ephesus write:
These are the words of him who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven golden lampstands:
NIV.2: I know your deeds, your hard work and your perseverance. I know that you cannot tolerate wicked men, that you have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and have found them false.
NIV.3: You have persevered and have endured hardships for my name, and have not grown weary.
NIV.4: Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken your first love.
NIV.5: Remember the height from which you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.
NIV.6: But you have this in your favor: You hate the practices of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
NIV.7: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.
NIV.8:
To the Church in Smyrna
“To the angel of the church in Smyrna write:
These are the words of him who is the First and the Last, who died and came to life again.
NIV.9: I know your afflictions and your poverty—yet you are rich! I know the slander of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
NIV.10: Do not be afraid of what you are about to suffer. I tell you, the devil will put some of you in prison to test you, and you will suffer persecution for ten days. Be faithful, even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
NIV.11: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. He who overcomes will not be hurt at all by the second death.
NIV.12:
To the Church in Pergamum
“To the angel of the church in Pergamum write:
These are the words of him who has the sharp, double-edged sword.
NIV.13: I know where you live—where Satan has his throne. Yet you remain true to my name. You did not renounce your faith in me, even in the days of Antipas, my faithful witness, who was put to death in your city—where Satan lives.
NIV.14: Nevertheless, I have a few things against you: You have people there who hold to the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to entice the Israelites to sin by eating food sacrificed to idols and by committing sexual immorality.
NIV.15: Likewise you also have those who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
NIV.16: Repent therefore! Otherwise, I will soon come to you and will fight against them with the sword of my mouth.
NIV.17: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes, I will give some of the hidden manna. I will also give him a white stone with a new name written on it, known only to him who receives it.
NIV.18:
To the Church in Thyatira
“To the angel of the church in Thyatira write:
These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes are like blazing fire and whose feet are like burnished bronze.
NIV.19: I know your deeds, your love and faith, your service and perseverance, and that you are now doing more than you did at first.
NIV.20: Nevertheless, I have this against you: You tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess. By her teaching she misleads my servants into sexual immorality and the eating of food sacrificed to idols.
NIV.21: I have given her time to repent of her immorality, but she is unwilling.
NIV.22: So I will cast her on a bed of suffering, and I will make those who commit adultery with her suffer intensely, unless they repent of her ways.
NIV.23: I will strike her children dead. Then all the churches will know that I am he who searches hearts and minds, and I will repay each of you according to your deeds.
NIV.24: Now I say to the rest of you in Thyatira, to you who do not hold to her teaching and have not learned Satan’s so-called deep secrets (I will not impose any other burden on you):
NIV.25: Only hold on to what you have until I come.
NIV.26: To him who overcomes and does my will to the end, I will give authority over the nations—
NIV.27:
‘He will rule them with an iron scepter;
he will dash them to pieces like pottery’—
just as I have received authority from my Father.
NIV.28: I will also give him the morning star.
NIV.29: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

NRSVCE.1: “To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: These are the words of him who holds the seven stars in his right hand, who walks among the seven golden lampstands:
NRSVCE.2: “I know your works, your toil and your patient endurance. I know that you cannot tolerate evildoers; you have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and have found them to be false.
NRSVCE.3: I also know that you are enduring patiently and bearing up for the sake of my name, and that you have not grown weary.
NRSVCE.4: But I have this against you, that you have abandoned the love you had at first.
NRSVCE.5: Remember then from what you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.
NRSVCE.6: Yet this is to your credit: you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
NRSVCE.7: Let anyone who has an ear listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. To everyone who conquers, I will give permission to eat from the tree of life that is in the paradise of God.
NRSVCE.8: “And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These are the words of the first and the last, who was dead and came to life:
NRSVCE.9: “I know your affliction and your poverty, even though you are rich. I know the slander on the part of those who say that they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
NRSVCE.10: Do not fear what you are about to suffer. Beware, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison so that you may be tested, and for ten days you will have affliction. Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life.
NRSVCE.11: Let anyone who has an ear listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Whoever conquers will not be harmed by the second death.
NRSVCE.12: “And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: These are the words of him who has the sharp two-edged sword:
NRSVCE.13: “I know where you are living, where Satan’s throne is. Yet you are holding fast to my name, and you did not deny your faith in me even in the days of Antipas my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan lives.
NRSVCE.14: But I have a few things against you: you have some there who hold to the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to put a stumbling block before the people of Israel, so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and practice fornication.
NRSVCE.15: So you also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
NRSVCE.16: Repent then. If not, I will come to you soon and make war against them with the sword of my mouth.
NRSVCE.17: Let anyone who has an ear listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. To everyone who conquers I will give some of the hidden manna, and I will give a white stone, and on the white stone is written a new name that no one knows except the one who receives it.
NRSVCE.18: “And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These are the words of the Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and whose feet are like burnished bronze:
NRSVCE.19: “I know your works — your love, faith, service, and patient endurance. I know that your last works are greater than the first.
NRSVCE.20: But I have this against you: you tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet and is teaching and beguiling my servants to practice fornication and to eat food sacrificed to idols.
NRSVCE.21: I gave her time to repent, but she refuses to repent of her fornication.
NRSVCE.22: Beware, I am throwing her on a bed, and those who commit adultery with her I am throwing into great distress, unless they repent of her doings;
NRSVCE.23: and I will strike her children dead. And all the churches will know that I am the one who searches minds and hearts, and I will give to each of you as your works deserve.
NRSVCE.24: But to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold this teaching, who have not learned what some call ‘the deep things of Satan,’ to you I say, I do not lay on you any other burden;
NRSVCE.25: only hold fast to what you have until I come.
NRSVCE.26: To everyone who conquers and continues to do my works to the end, I will give authority over the nations;
NRSVCE.27:
to rule them with an iron rod,
as when clay pots are shattered —
NRSVCE.28: even as I also received authority from my Father. To the one who conquers I will also give the morning star.
NRSVCE.29: Let anyone who has an ear listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.

NET.1: “To the angel of the church in Ephesus, write the following: “This is the solemn pronouncement of the one who has a firm grasp on the seven stars in his right hand — the one who walks among the seven golden lampstands:
NET.2: ‘I know your works as well as your labor and steadfast endurance, and that you cannot tolerate evil. You have even put to the test those who refer to themselves as apostles (but are not), and have discovered that they are false.
NET.3: I am also aware that you have persisted steadfastly, endured much for the sake of my name, and have not grown weary.
NET.4: But I have this against you: You have departed from your first love!
NET.5: Therefore, remember from what high state you have fallen and repent! Do the deeds you did at the first; if not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place — that is, if you do not repent.
NET.6: But you do have this going for you: You hate what the Nicolaitans practice — practices I also hate.
NET.7: The one who has an ear had better hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will permit him to eat from the tree of life that is in the paradise of God.’
NET.8: “To the angel of the church in Smyrna write the following: “This is the solemn pronouncement of the one who is the first and the last, the one who was dead, but came to life:
NET.9: ‘I know the distress you are suffering and your poverty (but you are rich). I also know the slander against you by those who call themselves Jews and really are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
NET.10: Do not be afraid of the things you are about to suffer. The devil is about to have some of you thrown into prison so you may be tested, and you will experience suffering for ten days. Remain faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown that is life itself.
NET.11: The one who has an ear had better hear what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who conquers will in no way be harmed by the second death.’
NET.12: “To the angel of the church in Pergamum write the following: “This is the solemn pronouncement of the one who has the sharp double-edged sword:
NET.13: ‘I know where you live — where Satan’s throne is. Yet you continue to cling to my name and you have not denied your faith in me, even in the days of Antipas, my faithful witness, who was killed in your city where Satan lives.
NET.14: But I have a few things against you: You have some people there who follow the teaching of Balaam, who instructed Balak to put a stumbling block before the people of Israel so they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
NET.15: In the same way, there are also some among you who follow the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
NET.16: Therefore, repent! If not, I will come against you quickly and make war against those people with the sword of my mouth.
NET.17: The one who has an ear had better hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will give him some of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, and on that stone will be written a new name that no one can understand except the one who receives it.’
NET.18: “To the angel of the church in Thyatira write the following: “This is the solemn pronouncement of the Son of God, the one who has eyes like a fiery flame and whose feet are like polished bronze:
NET.19: ‘I know your deeds: your love, faith, service, and steadfast endurance. In fact, your more recent deeds are greater than your earlier ones.
NET.20: But I have this against you: You tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, and by her teaching deceives my servants to commit sexual immorality and to eat food sacrificed to idols.
NET.21: I have given her time to repent, but she is not willing to repent of her sexual immorality.
NET.22: Look! I am throwing her onto a bed of violent illness, and those who commit adultery with her into terrible suffering, unless they repent of her deeds.
NET.23: Furthermore, I will strike her followers with a deadly disease, and then all the churches will know that I am the one who searches minds and hearts. I will repay each one of you what your deeds deserve.
NET.24: But to the rest of you in Thyatira, all who do not hold to this teaching (who have not learned the so-called “deep secrets of Satan”), to you I say: I do not put any additional burden on you.
NET.25: However, hold on to what you have until I come.
NET.26: And to the one who conquers and who continues in my deeds until the end, I will give him authority over the nations —
NET.27: he will rule them with an iron rod and like clay jars he will break them to pieces,
NET.28: just as I have received the right to rule from my Father — and I will give him the morning star.
NET.29: The one who has an ear had better hear what the Spirit says to the churches.’
